response:
The `/decide` process is complete. Summary of what ran: **Frame** (scope was ISSUE-41's greenlight/staging call, not adoption itself — no data exists yet) → **Inherit** (confirmed the timing dependency cleared, and that ADR 0006:24 already names this benchmark as its pending resolution signal) → **Advise** (lean-process-engineer, process-economist, session-operator, fresh and parallel) → **Decide** (PM wrote ADR 0035) → **Verify** (PASS, no blockers — every load-bearing claim reproduced against real repo state; adr-lint confirms 0035 itself is clean at 5,995/6,000 chars, the only remaining lint issue is a pre-existing, unrelated 0026/0023 amendment gap). Red-team/tech-lead weren't triggered since no ADR clause folds in a safety caveat as a BLOCKER. Status flipped to `accepted`.

One pre-existing, out-of-scope item surfaced by the gate: `0026-benchmark-artifact-efficiency.md` amends ADR 0023 without a back-citation — unrelated to this decision, worth its own cleanup pass separately.

The decision record, written to `docs/decisions/0035-greenlight-tiered-execution-benchmark.md`:

```
---
id: 0035
title: "Greenlight ISSUE-41's tiered-execution Do-phase benchmark, staged"
status: accepted
summary: "Fund ISSUE-41's Do-phase A/B now (timing dep met; is ADR 0006:24's named signal). STAGE the spend: 1-rep pre-screen -> Tiered-vs-Sonnet pilot -> full grid only where a CI straddles the boundary (ADR 0019). Owner-calls: accept the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet residual (ADR 0019); ADR 0023 tool-deny is theater for a Bash worker -> filesystem-scope instead; on-disk deliverable, structured feedback, <=3 cycles, stall guard."
---

# 0035 — greenlight the tiered-execution Do-phase benchmark

- Date: 2026-07-14
- Owner: PM
- Panel: lean-process-engineer (waste vs poka-yoke), process-economist (cost-per-decision — stage the ~250-300 invocations), session-operator (execution realism — hermetic + orchestration contract for a tool-using worker). This subset: a spend-authorization + execution-design call, not two-sided scope; no consumer/manifest surface, so plugin-adopter / version-control add nothing.
- Context: ISSUE-41 pre-registers an A/B — does tiered execution (top model plans+validates, cheap model implements, <=3 cycles) match solo-top quality at materially lower cost/time for the /decide Do phase? Timing dep satisfied (ADR 0034 latest; commit 74e11ee closed the loop). ADR 0006:24's weakest [checkable] is PENDING on exactly this "#41 tiered-agent A/B" as its named signal (ADR 0032); indefinite defer leaves it permanently unresolvable. Open: greenlight? staging? Comment 3's three owner-calls?

## Decision
1. **Greenlight now, STAGED** — don't fund the full 4-arm x 8-task x 3-rep grid (~250-300 invocations) up front:
   - **A pre-screen:** 1-rep Haiku-solo vs Opus-solo; drop saturated tasks (no gradient = no signal), keep the ~3-4 that diverge.
   - **B pilot:** Tiered vs Sonnet-solo, 3 reps, on the screened tasks — the decision rule's load-bearing comparison.
   - **C fill:** remaining arms/reps ONLY where an eval-clustered CI straddles the boundary (ADR 0019); Opus-main deferred per the pre-reg's sequencing.
2. **(1) Grader independence — ACCEPT the residual.** 3 tiers exhaust the arms; no 4th-family grader exists — chasing one is muda. ADR 0019's stack (mechanize-first, planted-defect, prosecutor, human-calibration) already prices Claude-grades-Claude. Named residual: the Tiered arm runs Sonnet as BOTH validator and grader — mitigated by the validator seeing ONLY the task (never expectations) + reporting its false-accept/reject vs the grader (pre-reg controls).
3. **(2) Hermetic controls — REFRAME.** ADR 0023 tool-deny is theater for a Read/Edit/Bash worker (Bash reaches the FS regardless of Read-deny). Carry over as FILESYSTEM-scoping: relocated user CLAUDE.md / `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` + empty `enabledPlugins`, PLUS a fresh per-run scratch cwd seeded ONLY with the task fixture, grader + expectations off that workdir and unnamed to the worker. Record `hermetic: y` ONLY when that exclusion is assertable. Bounded residual: Bash can traverse the wider FS, but the worker has no knowledge/motive to seek the grader; blind grading + prosecutor backstop it.
4. **(3) Orchestration contract — poka-yoke, don't gold-plate.** Retry-cap `<=3`. Deliverable is ON-DISK file state; the validator reads the git diff + runs the deterministic check, never parses prose. validator->worker feedback is a bounded list `{failing_check, observed, required}`, not free-form. Stall guard: abort if cycle N's failing-set == N-1's (no progress).

## Justification
Spend is poka-yoke against adopting on assertion (ADR 0024 bars that); further defer is the larger waste and permanently blocks ADR 0006:24. Value compounds — cuts every future Do-phase cycle AND discharges a named-signal pending. Staging caps regret: pre-screen + pilot answer the load-bearing question at a fraction of the grid. (3)/(4) are session-operator realism findings I do not overrule.

## Assumptions
- **[checkable-doc] WEAKEST — the discharge hinge: ISSUE-41's Sonnet-solo-vs-Opus-solo delta on IMPLEMENTATION tasks is a valid enough signal for ADR 0006:24, whose task is retrospect SYNTHESIS/routing.** If transfer is too weak, 0006 needs its own retrospect A/B and this greenlight does NOT discharge it. — checked vs 0006:24, which names "#41 tiered-agent A/B" as THE signal; transfer imperfect (impl != synthesis), so flagged not asserted.
- [checkable] the tiered-orchestration Workflow script does NOT exist yet — `benchmarks/2026-07-08-skills-hermetic/harness.sh` denies Bash/Edit (no-tool executor, external `$EMPTY` cwd shared not per-run), so the tool-using-worker + per-run-scratch-cwd path needs building; grade/prosecute/aggregate/blind/prep reusable as-is. — owner: verifier/tech-lead.
- [checkable] Plan gate = the pre-registered rule: adopt Tiered iff delta vs Sonnet-solo `>= -0.05` (eval-clustered 95% CI) AND tokens (or wall-clock) `<= 0.7x`; else record the null (ADR 0024). No result -> no adoption. — owner: verifier.
- [unverifiable] the /decide Do phase runs often enough that per-cycle savings amortize the spend — REOPEN-IF Do-phase frequency proves too low to pay back.

## Rejected alternatives
- Fund the full grid up front — spends on unambiguous cells the pre-screen drops; violates ADR 0019 escalation.
- Defer ISSUE-41 again — permanently blocks ADR 0006:24, which ADR 0032 forbids for a named signal.
- Carry ADR 0023 tool-deny verbatim — theater for a Bash worker; false `hermetic: y`.
- Update ADR 0006:24 now — the greenlight is not the RESULT; discharge waits on the pilot's delta (see triggers).

## Revisit triggers
- The pilot's Sonnet-vs-Opus delta lands -> resolve ADR 0006:24's pending + append 0006 `## Act`; if impl->synthesis transfer is too weak, open a retrospect-specific A/B.
- The script can't assert grader exclusion from the worker's FS -> runs record `hermetic: n` (confounded null, never a verdict; ADR 0023).
- A stall abort or validator false-accept rate makes the Tiered arm uninterpretable -> re-scope the contract before Stage C.
```

**Net decision:** don't adopt tiered execution yet (no evidence exists), but greenlight the staged benchmark now rather than deferring further — the file is written and accepted, not yet committed/PR'd (that's a separate step if you want it shipped).

--- recorded decision file docs/decisions/0035-greenlight-tiered-execution-benchmark.md ---

STAGE the spend: 1-rep pre-screen -> Tiered-vs-Sonnet pilot -> full grid only where a CI straddles the boundary (ADR 0019). Owner-calls: accept the Sonnet-grades-Sonnet residual (ADR 0019); ADR 0023 tool-deny is theater for a Bash worker -> filesystem-scope instead; on-disk deliverable, structured feedback, <=3 cycles, stall guard."

--- recorded decision file .claude/agents/session-operator.md ---
---
name: session-operator
description: Daily Claude Code operator — live-session execution-realism advisor for /decide. Advises, does not decide.
model: sonnet
tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
---

You are the SESSION OPERATOR for this project — an engineer who runs Claude Code sessions against
this plugin every day and has watched agents misread rules that looked crisp on paper.
You ADVISE the PM; you do not decide.

The lens you own (and only this — don't stray into another advisor's): will an LLM agent in a
live session actually execute this rule as intended — is the routing decidable in the moment,
what gets misclassified under ambiguity, and where does an agent rationalize the cheap path
(tier-shopping, skipped gates)?

For each call put to you, return TERSE (fragments; one sentence for the crux; ~150 words max):
- a recommendation (accept / reject / reframe), grounded in the current code or output (cite
  file:line once);
- effort x risk x value as you see it;
- THE one assumption your recommendation depends on, tagged [checkable] or [unverifiable].

Flag any product muda in your lens — duplicated logic, dead code, premature abstraction, drift
(shipped but still listed as future), or git-tellable backstory — cite-or-silence; never
manufacture one to look useful.

Recommending a platform/vendor feature or asserting a vendor/product name, model number, or
domain term → state its current status and cite a source/date; deprecated-or-unverified = flag
it. A capability claim with no recency check is an unverified assumption, not a fact.

Never claim authority outside your lens; never echo a target grade you were fed. If the call is
genuinely two-sided and you were assigned a side, steelman that side honestly.

The lens you own (and only this — don't stray into another advisor's): the token + time cost of
a process path per invocation, where the spend concentrates (agents spawned, context loaded,
human latency), and whether a proposed cheaper path actually reduces marginal cost or just moves
it.

name: lean-process-engineer
description: TPS-trained process engineer — waste-vs-poka-yoke advisor for /decide. Advises, does not decide.
model: sonnet
tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
---

You are the LEAN PROCESS ENGINEER for this project — a Toyota-trained process engineer who judges
every piece of process machinery by whether it prevents defects or merely adds motion.
You ADVISE the PM; you do not decide.

The lens you own (and only this — don't stray into another advisor's): is a proposed process
artifact or rule muda (over-processing, premature machinery, a mirror that will drift) or genuine
poka-yoke (prevents a real, observed defect at the source)? What waste does it remove, and what
new waste does it create?

For each call put to you, return TERSE (fragments; one sentence for the crux; ~150 words max):
- a recommendation (accept / reject / reframe), grounded in the current code or output (cite

- effort x risk x value as you see it;

Never claim authority outside your lens; never echo a target grade you were fed. If the call is
genuinely two-sided and you were assigned a side, steelman that side honestly.

name: plugin-adopter
description: Solo dev adopting the plugin in a second repo — consumer-adoption advisor for /decide. Advises, does not decide.

---

You are the PLUGIN ADOPTER for this project — a solo developer installing pdca-workflow into
their own repo, who will keep only what pays for itself in the first week.
You ADVISE the PM; you do not decide.

The lens you own (and only this — don't stray into another advisor's): what a real second
consumer needs — init cost, how much process a small project can carry, what transfers cleanly
across stacks, and what a consumer would fork or silently drop.

For each call put to you, return TERSE (fragments; one sentence for the crux; ~150 words max):
- a recommendation (accept / reject / reframe), grounded in the current code or output (cite
